display-name.lang: United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Bizarrely-shaped huts in India, once home to global superstars, are now eerily quiet and neglected, and a shattered compound in Colombia linked to a criminal mastermind.

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: The home of an African dictator and a sporting hero - and the facility that held apartheid's greatest critic - are abandoned but still have unique stories to tell.

programme.title: Great American Railroad Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo explores the Province of Quebec, taking the Charlevoix train along the bank of the St Lawrence River to La Malbaie.

programme.title: Great American Railroad Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo strikes west across Manitoba into the province of Saskatchewan.

programme.title: Great American Railroad Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo sets on his final leg, all the way to Jasper, along the way admiring Edmonton's light rail system and visiting the Rocky Mountains.

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: Life in some of the many deserts and grasslands around the world, including in the Namib desert, where ostriches raise their family in the searing heat.

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: David Attenborough journeys into the hidden world of forests, from the temperate rainforests of Canada, to the teak forests of India.

programme.title: Mammals
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a look into the lives of mammals and how they have adapted to their surroundings. The first episode features animals that thrive in the dark.

programme.title: Mammals
programme.desc: A look at how mammals adapt to the changes humans have made to their environment.

programme.title: Mammals
programme.desc: How air-breathing mammals have found remarkable ways to overcome the many challenges of a life in water, from freshwater jungle ponds to the dark depths of the open ocean.

programme.title: Mammals
programme.desc: From ice-covered seas to snow-capped mountains, mammals have conquered the cold, living in the harshest places on earth thanks to their intelligence and adaptations.

programme.title: Mammals
programme.desc: David Attenborough reveals the ways mammals survive the hottest places on earth, from camels storing water in humps to sijaka lemurs hugging trees.

programme.title: Mammals
programme.desc: David Attenborough reveals why forests are the ultimate test of survival for mammals, including a tiger who is put to the test in the heat of an Indian summer.

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Paul, Mick and Charlie are in Hull for one of their biggest ever collections. Dave takes a test drive in Tesla Model X and Derek is bowled over by a classic vintage mo.

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: The Mathewson family business starts a new era as Charlie heads off on his first solo collection, to pick up an Austin van from the North York Moors.

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek picks up some very special working vehicles. Charlie feels the pull of a gargantuan Hummer and Paul extracts a vintage tractor from a Yorkshire barn.

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: David Renwick's masterly comic creation, Victor Meldrew. Roger, Victor's cousin, accidentally invites the couple to his 60th birthday. Big mistake.

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: Why can't the Meldrews just have a nice pleasant day out? Naturally, a trip out on the river leads to epic complications for Victor and his long-suffering spouse.

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: More great, dark comedy as the Meldrews are back from holiday and find that their neighbours Patrick (aka Angus Deayton) and Pippa have put their house on the market.

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Charlie picks up an iconic 60s motor - a Ford Cortina Deluxe - and a 1970s Morris van brings a taste of Carry On Camping to Pickering.

programme.title: Robson Green's Weekend Escapes
programme.desc: Actress Charlie Hardwick shows Robson the hidden gems of the east Durham coastline while taking an art class on the sand and attending an outdoor singing session.

programme.title: Robson Green's Weekend Escapes
programme.desc: Robson is joined by author LJ Ross on a trip along the Northumbrian coast - they visit Holy Island, the setting for LJ's first novel, and sign up for a Nordic walking lesson.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from Home.

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Exploring a ghost town in Colorado, the remnants of an industrial enterprise in Puerto Rico, and a remote village on Cyprus.

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Exploring a jungle refuge concealing a story of royal deceit, nuclear bunkers infiltrated by outsiders, and a haunted hospital marked by scandal.

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek gives the once-over to a Royale Formula Ford race car, and Paul is on a marathon trip to pick up a 1953 MGYB, a Moggy Van, and an '80s Yamaha 250RD motorbike.

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Thunderbird / Citroen SM / Punto / BMW: Derek travels to the Lancashire coast to pick up a rare 1972 Citroen SM, and Dave admires a two-decade-old Punto with just 42 miles on the clock.

programme.title: The Men and the Machines That Beat Hitler
programme.desc: Mark Urban continues the story of men from the Fifth Royal Tank Regiment. This moving final episode covers D-Day, the battle for Normandy and eventual victory in Germany.

programme.title: D-Day To Berlin
programme.desc: Anglo-American relations toward the end of 1944 began to sour as the Allies fought in the conference chamber for the right to direct the final assault on Germany.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The experts visit the North Yorkshire town of Scarborough, where finds include a carved tribute to a Spitfire pilot killed in action.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents from Farnborough, Hampshire. Items include early designs for a flying machine and a letter written in the First World War trenches.

programme.title: Bangers & Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The team break with tradition and actually follow Dave Mathewson's advice on what to restore - he points to a 1992 Vauxhall Nova Merit Plus.

programme.title: Bangers & Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The team restore a 1988 Golf GTI Convertible - an iconic hot hatch - along with a wooden launch boat and a catch-up with a Mk1 Austin Mini.

programme.title: The Men and the Machines That Beat Hitler
programme.desc: Mark Urban continues the story of men from the Fifth Royal Tank Regiment. This moving final episode covers D-Day, the battle for Normandy and eventual victory in Germany.

programme.title: D-Day To Berlin
programme.desc: Anglo-American relations toward the end of 1944 began to sour as the Allies fought in the conference chamber for the right to direct the final assault on Germany.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The experts visit the North Yorkshire town of Scarborough, where finds include a carved tribute to a Spitfire pilot killed in action.

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: David Attenborough journeys into the hidden world of forests, from the temperate rainforests of Canada, to the teak forests of India.

programme.title: 1066: A Year to Conquer England
programme.desc: After King Harold's brother Tostig attacks the south coast, another enemy strikes when a vast Viking force lands in the north. Harold rushes to fight for his kingdom - and his life.

programme.title: Empire with Michael Portillo
programme.desc: Michael travels to India to uncover how the British East India Company - the world's first multinational corporation - raised a private army to create an empire.

programme.title: Bangers & Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The team break with tradition and actually follow Dave Mathewson's advice on what to restore - he points to a 1992 Vauxhall Nova Merit Plus.

programme.title: Bangers & Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The team restore a 1988 Golf GTI Convertible - an iconic hot hatch - along with a wooden launch boat and a catch-up with a Mk1 Austin Mini.
